meta.title: Plan a surprise 30th birthday party
meta.description: A lead agent splits a party into three briefs, hands each to a helper with its own small window, and composes their summaries — adapting when one comes back over budget and another spoils the surprise.

tools.name: search_web
tools.description: Search the web for pages relevant to a query.

tools.name: fetch_page
tools.description: Open a page and read its text.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write a file to the shared workspace.

sources.title: Garden Room — private dining at Fig & Vine

events.summary: You are a coordinating agent. You can search the web, read pages, and write files — and you can hand a focused sub-task to a helper agent, each of which has its own small context window and reports back a short summary.

events.text: Plan a surprise 30th birthday for my sister, three weeks out. About 25 people. She loves Thai food and hates being the center of attention.

This is really three jobs — a venue, the food, and the invitations. If I research all of it in one window it fills up fast, and I start dropping the early details. I'll split it: one helper per job, each with a short brief and its own fresh window. All I need back is their conclusions.

events.task: Find a private venue for a surprise 30th birthday — about 25 guests, three weeks out. It has to feel low-key: she hates being the center of attention, so no big open restaurant floor. Report the best option with its capacity and cost.
Find a caterer for a 30th birthday, about 25 people, three weeks out. She loves Thai food. Report one option with a per-person price.

Draft a short invitation for a surprise 30th birthday, about 25 guests, three weeks out. Keep the surprise: ask guests to arrive 30 minutes early and stay quiet. She hates being the center of attention, so keep the tone warm and low-key.

events.text: A private room or a small loft — not a table on a restaurant floor. Let me search for what's bookable.

results.title: Sunset Loft — private rooftop room
results.snippet: Private rooftop room, seats up to 30. Four-hour hire.

results.title: Garden Room — private dining at Fig & Vine
results.snippet: Intimate back room for private parties, seats 26.

results.title: The Grand Hall — banquet venue
results.snippet: Our main ballroom seats 300 under the spotlights.

events.text: Thai food for 25. I want a caterer that can set up a buffet, not a sit-down table. Let me search.

results.title: Bangkok House Catering
results.snippet: Thai party buffets for groups. Per-head pricing.

results.title: Thai Orchid — dine-in restaurant
results.snippet: Sit-down Thai restaurant, bookings for up to 12.

results.title: Spice Boat — Thai meal kits
results.snippet: Thai meal kits delivered; you cook it yourself.

events.text: It has to be warm, low-key, and protect the surprise. I'll ask guests to come early and keep it quiet.

events.extracted: Sunset Loft — private rooftop room, seats up to 30. Hire covers four hours. Minimum spend $2,400 on food and drink.
Bangkok House Catering — party buffet for groups, $28 per person, we set up and clear away. Optional birthday surprise: a gong and a table announcement to the whole room.

events.text: Draft: 'Surprise 30th for [name]! Please arrive by 6:30 — she arrives at 7. Keep it quiet, and come ready to celebrate.'
The loft fits and it's private, but a $2,400 minimum is a lot for 25 people once food is separate. I'll flag it — the budget is the lead's call, not mine.

events.summary: Top option: Sunset Loft — a private rooftop room, seats up to 30, feels nicely low-key. Catch: it carries a $2,400 minimum food-and-drink spend, which is likely over budget once catering is counted separately. I haven't checked the second option yet.

events.text: I only see venue's summary, not its whole search — and it says the loft is over budget. I don't want the whole budget going to a room minimum. I'll re-brief venue: flat-fee rooms only, no minimum spend.

events.summary: Recommended: Bangkok House Catering — a Thai buffet at $28 a head (about $700 for 25). Bonus: they throw in a birthday surprise — a gong and an announcement to the whole room.

events.text: Good price on the food — but 'a gong and an announcement to the whole room' is exactly what she'd hate. Food didn't know that; I never put it in its brief. That's on me. I'll keep the caterer and just decline the announcement.

events.summary: Draft invite: 'Surprise 30th for [name]! Please arrive by 6:30 — she gets there at 7. Keep it quiet: no big fuss, just the people she loves. Come celebrate.'

events.task: Revised brief: find a private room with a flat hire fee and no minimum spend — about 25 guests, low-key. Ignore anything with a minimum spend over $500.

events.extracted: Garden Room at Fig & Vine — private back room, seats 26. Flat $300 room fee, no minimum spend. Outside catering welcome.

events.text: Flat $300, no minimum, fits 26, and we can bring our own caterer. That's the one.

events.summary: Revised pick: the Garden Room — a private back room at a bistro. Seats 26, flat $300 fee, no minimum spend, and outside catering is allowed. Low-key and private.

events.text: Now I have all three: the Garden Room ($300, private), Bangkok House ($28 a head, no announcement), and a quiet invite. Venue and food land around $1,000 for 25. That fits. I'll write it up.

files.party-plan.md:
# Surprise 30th — the plan

Guest of honor: your sister · Guests: ~25 · When: 3 weeks out

## Venue — Garden Room
Private back room at Fig & Vine. Seats 26, flat $300 room fee, no minimum
spend, outside catering allowed. Quiet and private — no restaurant floor,
no spotlight.

## Food — Bangkok House Catering
Thai buffet, $28 per person (~$700 for 25). We declined their birthday
gong-and-announcement — she doesn't want to be the center of attention.

## Invitations
"Surprise 30th for [name]! Please arrive by 6:30 (she arrives at 7). Keep
it quiet — no big fuss, just the people she loves."

## Rough budget
Venue $300 + food ~$700 = ~$1,000, before cake and decorations.

events.text: Here's the plan. Venue: the Garden Room — a private bistro back room that seats 26, a flat $300 fee, no minimum spend, and it lets us bring our own caterer. Food: Bangkok House, a Thai buffet at about $28 a head (~$700 for 25); I turned down their birthday announcement, since she'd hate being put on the spot. Invitations: a short, low-key note asking everyone to arrive by 6:30 and keep it quiet. Venue and food come to about $1,000. I've written it all up in party-plan.md.
